Title: The MdBBX22–miR858–MdMYB9/11/12 module regulates proanthocyanidin biosynthesis in apple peel

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Apple peel; increases during peel development as PA levels decline; induced under light stress
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Apple peel during fruit development; induced by light stress
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter; native mdm-miR858 promoter in reporter assays
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Apple fruit peel; also assayed in apple calli, tobacco flowers, and Arabidopsis seeds
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Dietary antioxidants beneficial to human health, with antioxidation, anti-inflammation and antitumour-related benefits
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates PA accumulation by cleaving and repressing MdMYB9, MdMYB11 and MdMYB12; under light stress indirectly promotes anthocyanin accumulation by suppressing the PA branch
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. mdm-miR858 expression was negatively correlated with total PA content during apple peel development. 2. 5'-RACE, GUS degradation and transient luciferase assays showed that mdm-miR858 cleaves and inhibits MdMYB9/11/12. 3. Overexpression of mdm-miR858 reduced catechin, epicatechin, procyanidin B1, procyanidin B2 and total PAs in apple calli, tobacco and Arabidopsis backgrounds expressing MdMYB9/11/12. 4. Under light stress, mdm-miR858 contributed to reduced PA accumulation and enhanced anthocyanin synthesis in peel.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: sRNA-seq correlation analysis; RT-qPCR; 5'-RACE; GUS protein degradation assay; transient dual-luciferase assay; overexpression in apple calli, tobacco and Arabidopsis; metabolite profiling by HPLC and DMACA staining; STTM inhibition in apple peel
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit development stage; continuous light stress after bag removal at 120 DAFB
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functions post-transcriptionally as a negative regulator of the PA branch; STTM858-mediated inhibition of miR858 increased PAs and decreased anthocyanins; effect is linked to metabolic competition between PA and anthocyanin branches under light stress
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to tune flavonoid partitioning in apple peel: suppress miR858 or use target mimicry to raise PA content; elevate miR858 to favor anthocyanin coloration under light. Deployment should consider the tradeoff between PA-based antioxidant profile and red coloration.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Increasing mdm-miR858 lowers PA accumulation while increasing anthocyanin under light stress; suppressing mdm-miR858 increases PAs but can reduce anthocyanin coloration

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in young peel when PA accumulation is high; low under light stress in peel
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Apple peel and apple calli; heterologous assays in tobacco flowers and Arabidopsis seeds
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Dietary antioxidant flavonoids beneficial to human health
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes PA accumulation; its effect is suppressed by mdm-miR858
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Expression pattern was opposite to mdm-miR858 and consistent with PA accumulation. 2. Overexpression increased catechin, epicatechin, procyanidin B1, procyanidin B2 and total PAs in apple calli. 3. Silencing reduced total PAs, and combined silencing with MdMYB11/12 caused a stronger reduction, indicating functional redundancy. 4. Overexpression in tobacco and Arabidopsis promoted PA accumulation, but coexpression of mdm-miR858 suppressed these effects.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; transient dual-luciferase assay with MdANR promoter; overexpression and silencing in apple calli/peel; heterologous overexpression in tobacco and Arabidopsis; HPLC and DMACA staining
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts redundantly with MdMYB11 and MdMYB12 in PA regulation; targeted by mdm-miR858 cleavage
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate positive regulator to increase PA content, but may need miR858-resistant editing or stacking with other PA regulators for robust effect.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In tobacco, overexpression increased PAs but reduced anthocyanin content, indicating branch competition

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in young peel when PA accumulation is high; decreased under light stress in peel
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Apple peel and apple calli; heterologous assays in tobacco flowers and Arabidopsis seeds
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Dietary antioxidant flavonoids beneficial to human health
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes PA accumulation; its effect is suppressed by mdm-miR858 and by MdBBX22 via induction of mdm-miR858
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Expression was negatively correlated with mdm-miR858 and positively correlated with PA content. 2. Overexpression increased PA accumulation in apple calli, tobacco and Arabidopsis. 3. Silencing reduced PAs, with redundancy relative to MdMYB9/12. 4. mdm-miR858 and light-induced MdBBX22 suppressed MdMYB11 expression and PA accumulation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; 5'-RACE target validation; transient dual-luciferase assay with MdANR promoter; overexpression/silencing; HPLC and DMACA staining
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: mdm-miR858; MdbHLH33; MdbHLH3; MdBBX22 (indirect through miR858)
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of the principal PA-promoting MYBs repressed under light stress through the MdBBX22–miR858 module
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Positive target for PA enhancement, but repression by miR858 under light should be considered; combining with miR858-resistant alleles may improve stability.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced PA branch can reduce anthocyanin accumulation under some conditions

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in young peel when PA accumulation is high; decreased under light stress in peel
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Apple peel and apple calli; heterologous assays in tobacco flowers and Arabidopsis seeds
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Dietary antioxidant flavonoids beneficial to human health
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes PA accumulation; its effect is suppressed by mdm-miR858 and by MdBBX22 via induction of mdm-miR858
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Expression pattern matched PA accumulation and opposed mdm-miR858. 2. Overexpression increased PA levels in apple calli and complemented the Arabidopsis tt2 mutant for seed PA accumulation. 3. mdm-miR858 cleaved and repressed MdMYB12. 4. A miR858-resistant synonymous mutant (MdMYB12m) partially escaped MdBBX22-mediated inhibition, showing the importance of the miR858 target site.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; 5'-RACE; GUS assay; transient dual-luciferase assay with MdLAR promoter; overexpression/silencing; mutant miR858-resistant allele; HPLC and DMACA staining
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: mdm-miR858; MdbHLH33; MdbHLH3; MdBBX22 (indirect through miR858)
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MdMYB12m contains five synonymous mutations in the miR858 target site and attenuates the inhibitory effect of MdBBX22, supporting direct miR858 control
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for elevating PA content; editing the miR858 target site without changing protein sequence is a promising strategy for stable PA enhancement.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Boosting PA branch may decrease anthocyanin accumulation and fruit coloration under light stress

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in apple peel under light stress; overexpressed in calli and peel in functional assays
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter; mdm-miR858 native promoter in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Apple fruit peel and apple calli
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Dietary antioxidant flavonoids beneficial to human health
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Inhibits PA accumulation by activating mdm-miR858, which represses MdMYB9/11/12; under light stress this shift indirectly enhances anthocyanin synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Yeast one-hybrid screening identified MdBBX22 as a protein binding the mdm-miR858 promoter. 2. Transient LUC assays, Y1H, EMSA and ChIP-PCR demonstrated that MdBBX22 binds the G-box in the mdm-miR858 promoter and activates its expression. 3. Overexpression of MdBBX22 in MdMYB9/11/12-overexpressing apple calli increased mdm-miR858, lowered MdMYB9/11/12 expression and reduced PA accumulation. 4. Under light stress in apple peel, MdBBX22 promoted mdm-miR858 expression, reduced PAs and increased anthocyanin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates mdm-miR858 transcription; indirectly represses MdMYB9, MdMYB11 and MdMYB12 and downstream PA genes MdLAR1/2 and MdANR1/2
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Branch selection within flavonoid metabolism, shifting flux away from proanthocyanidin synthesis toward anthocyanin accumulation under light stress
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation through direct promoter binding to G-box motif
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Light stress / continuous light after bag removal
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Light-responsive upstream controller of the miR858–MYB module that suppresses PA accumulation and promotes anthocyanin-favoring flux redistribution under light stress
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast one-hybrid screening; transient dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; ChIP-PCR; overexpression and silencing in apple peel/calli; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: mdm-miR858 promoter G-box; mdm-miR858; MdMYB9; MdMYB11; MdMYB12
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Continuous light stress after bag removal at 120 DAFB
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A miR858-resistant MdMYB12m partially alleviated MdBBX22-mediated inhibition of PA accumulation, confirming the functional importance of the MdBBX22→miR858→MYB12 route
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for improving peel coloration under light by enhancing anthocyanin accumulation, but activation may reduce PA content. Fine-tuning or tissue/time-specific expression may help balance nutritional PA content and marketable color.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression reduces PA accumulation while increasing anthocyanins, indicating nutritional/color tradeoff

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Young apple peel and transgenic apple calli; expression pattern positively correlated with PAs
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Apple peel; apple calli; tobacco flowers; Arabidopsis seeds
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Dietary antioxidants beneficial to human health
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates PA biosynthesis and increases PA accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdMYB9 was a direct target of mdm-miR858 cleavage. 2. MdMYB9 activated PA-related structural gene expression with bHLH cofactors and increased PAs when overexpressed. 3. Silencing decreased total PA content. 4. mdm-miR858 overexpression antagonized MdMYB9-induced PA accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates PA biosynthetic genes, especially MdANR and MdLAR, with MdbHLH33/MdbHLH3
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive PA-specific MYB regulator contributing redundantly to PA branch activation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 5'-RACE; transient LUC assay; overexpression/silencing; HPLC; DMACA staining
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functionally redundant with MdMYB11 and MdMYB12
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Overexpression or miR858-resistant editing could raise peel PA levels, but may reduce anthocyanin-driven coloration.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced PA accumulation can coincide with lower anthocyanin content

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Young peel; reduced under light stress and by mdm-miR858/MdBBX22 activity
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Apple peel; apple calli; tobacco flowers; Arabidopsis seeds
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Dietary antioxidants beneficial to human health
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates PA biosynthesis and is negatively controlled by mdm-miR858 and MdBBX22
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdMYB11 is a direct mdm-miR858 target. 2. Overexpression increased PAs and induced downstream structural genes. 3. Silencing reduced PAs. 4. Under light stress, MdBBX22-mediated induction of mdm-miR858 lowered MdMYB11 expression and PA accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates PA biosynthetic genes including MdANR and MdLAR with MdbHLH33/MdbHLH3
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important positive regulator of PA accumulation in peel, especially relevant under light-responsive repression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 5'-RACE; transient LUC assay; overexpression/silencing; HPLC; DMACA staining; transient peel assays
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of the main PA regulators downregulated in peel under light stress
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be manipulated to increase PAs, but regulation by light-responsive miR858 should be considered.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Increasing this PA regulator may oppose anthocyanin accumulation

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Young peel; reduced under light stress and by mdm-miR858/MdBBX22
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Apple peel; apple calli; tobacco flowers; Arabidopsis seeds
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Dietary antioxidants beneficial to human health
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates PA biosynthesis and is negatively controlled by mdm-miR858 and MdBBX22
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdMYB12 is directly cleaved by mdm-miR858. 2. Overexpression promoted PA accumulation and activated MdLAR. 3. A miR858-resistant version (MdMYB12m) weakened the inhibitory effect of MdBBX22 on PA accumulation. 4. Silencing or miR858-mediated repression reduced PAs and favored anthocyanin accumulation under light stress.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates PA biosynthetic genes including MdLAR and MdANR with MdbHLH33/MdbHLH3
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive PA regulator whose miR858 target site is functionally decisive for light-responsive PA suppression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 5'-RACE; transient LUC assay; overexpression/silencing; miR858-resistant synonymous mutant; HPLC; DMACA staining; transient peel assays
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MdMYB12m demonstrates that preserving protein function while disrupting miR858 targeting can sustain PA accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent candidate for precision editing of the miR858 target site to stabilize PA enrichment without altering the encoded protein.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Sustained PA accumulation may reduce anthocyanin-based peel coloration under light stress
